name: Dumbbell Curtsy Lunge
resistance_profile: Crossover lunge that loads the glute medius in a lengthened, adducted position at the bottom; hardest through the mid-range drive back to standing.
cues: Sit straight down, don't lean out over the front leg
Keep the front shin vertical and the hips square
Drive through the front heel to stand tall
execution_steps: Stand tall holding dumbbells at your sides (or one at the chest), feet hip-width.
Step one foot back and across behind the other, like a curtsy, keeping the front shin vertical.
Bend both knees to lower straight down until the front thigh is near parallel.
Drive through the front heel to stand, returning the rear foot to the start.
Keep the hips square throughout, completing the reps on one side or alternating.
good_when: You want to emphasize the glute medius and add frontal-plane variety
You want single-leg hypertrophy with dumbbells only
You want to train hip stability under load
bad_when: The crossover position bothers your knees
Your balance or coordination isn't ready (regress first)
You want to load very heavy
common_errors: Letting the front knee cave inward
Twisting the hips instead of keeping them square
Reaching the back foot too far and losing balance
progressions: Add a slow eccentric or a small deficit
regressions: Switch to a reverse or standard lunge while building coordination
